data.body:
G3 container coverage audit (parent f89d-e54d-203d-4ee0 × child 33d0 "Collapse dual outbound applier paths"), cross-checked against the full sibling roster.

This child FULLY covers parent AC3 ("dual outbound applier paths collapsed to one; retired path deleted") and parent SC2 ("outbound exercised through the production code path, not a parallel dead path") — these are its central deliverables. It also contributes to AC7/SC5 (suite green; asserts effects not mock-call-counts). All other parent criteria it touches are correctly delegated to named siblings (AC4/SC3→2359 telemetry; AC1/AC2/SC1→8937 schema+contract, fixtures fe57; AC5/SC4→822a round-trip/probe), so no per-criterion gap is attributable to this child.

One ROSTER-LEVEL absence surfaced during the audit (not this child's fault, but reportable per G3):
- finding: Parent AC6 ("A documented anti-change-detector guardrail GOVERNS new tests") appears uncovered as a standalone deliverable. Each child (33d0 AC4, 2359 AC4, 8937 AC4, 822a AC4, 59ef AC3, fe57) only APPLIES the anti-change-detector posture to its OWN tests; no sibling owns a criterion that produces the documented governing guardrail/policy artifact itself.
  - criteria: parent AC6
  - location: parent f89d-e54d-203d-4ee0 Acceptance Criteria bullet 6; cross-checked against siblings 2359/33d0/59ef/822a/8937/fe57
  - evidence: child 33d0 AC4 "asserts effects … not interaction/mock-call-counts" (self-scoped); 2359 AC4, 8937 AC4, 822a AC4, 59ef AC3 likewise self-scoped to each story's own tests; none defines a documented guardrail that governs FUTURE/new tests
  - scenarios: a new reconciler test added next cycle has no documented guardrail to conform to; the anti-change-detector intent silently erodes
  - impact: parent AC6 cannot be checked off by closing the current children as written (4-bucket: PARTIALLY covered — posture applied per-test, documented governing artifact absent)
  - checklist_item: union of children covers each parent acceptance/success criterion
  - suggested_fix: add the documented-guardrail deliverable to one story (natural home: 8937, the contract keystone) or add a small dedicated child, so AC6's documented governing artifact has an owner.

No coverage finding against child 33d0 itself for AC3/SC2; child is consistent with its declared scope (telemetry explicitly out → story E/2359).
